items.content_html: This plugin for Obsidian is designed to enhance your journaling experience by providing random prompts to inspire creativity and enrich your journal entries.

items.content_html: An Obsidian plugin that fixes require() calls, supporting JavaScript and TypeScript modules, enabling easy invocation, and adding code buttons for enhanced scripting capabilities.
items.title: mnaoumov/obsidian-fix-require-modules: An Obsidian plugin that fixes require() calls, supporting JavaScript and TypeScript modules, enabling easy invocation, and adding code buttons for enhanced scripting capabilities.

items.content_html: EnhancedCodeblockDiff is an Obsidian plugin that enables simultaneous diff display and syntax highlighting within code blocks. With this plugin, you can visually identify code changes while preserving language-specific syntax coloring, making code comparison and review more efficient.
items.title: Syu-fu/obsidian-enhanced-codeblock-diff: EnhancedCodeblockDiff is an Obsidian plugin that enables simultaneous diff display and syntax highlighting within code blocks. With this plugin, you can visually identify code changes while preserving language-specific syntax coloring, making code comparison and review more efficient.

items.content_html: Find Orphaned Images is an Obsidian plugin designed to help you keep your vault clean and organized by identifying and managing images that are not linked anywhere in your notes.
items.title: josmarcristello/Obsidian-Find-Orphaned-Images: Find Orphaned Images is an Obsidian plugin designed to help you keep your vault clean and organized by identifying and managing images that are not linked anywhere in your notes.

items.content_html: This Obsidian plugin generates a linked file tree of a specified folder, with options to use either Obsidian URLs (default) or relative paths. The output can be inserted directly into the current note.
items.title: mckls/obsidian-file-tree-generator: This Obsidian plugin generates a linked file tree of a specified folder, with options to use either Obsidian URLs (default) or relative paths. The output can be inserted directly into the current note.
